Formula Sheet
⚛ Nuclear Physics
▶
Δm = Zmₕ + (A−Z)mₙ − M (mass defect)
BE = Δm × 931.5 MeV/u (binding energy)
E = mc² (mass-energy equivalence)
A = λN (activity)
t½ = 0.693/λ (half-life)
N = N₀ × e⁻𝛌𝑡 (radioactive decay)
🎯 Cross Sections & Reactions
▶
σ microscopic cross section; units: barns (1b = 10⁻²⁴ cm²)
Σ = Nσ macroscopic cross section; units: cm⁻¹
λ = 1/Σ (mean free path)
φ = nv (neutron flux)
R = Σφ = Nσφ (reaction rate)
σa = σf + σc (absorption = fission + capture)
☢ Criticality & Reactivity
▶
k∞ = εpfη (four factor formula)
keff = k∞ × Lf × Lth (six factor formula)
ρ = (keff − 1)/keff (reactivity)
1 Nile = 1%Δk/k = 0.01
1 Dollar = βeff
M = 1/(1 − keff) (subcritical multiplication)
CR = S₀ × M (count rate)
⏱ Reactor Kinetics
▶
τ = 26.06/SUR (reactor period)
Pt = P₀ × 10^(SUR×t) (power from SUR)
DT = 18/SUR (doubling time, approx)
P = P₀ × βeff/(βeff − ρ) (prompt drop/jump)
βeff ≈ 0.0065 (U-235); decreases with burnup
🌡 Temperature & Reactivity Coefficients
▶
FTC (Doppler): always negative; |FTC| decreases at higher temps
MTC: negative in undermoderated; can be positive in overmoderated
Power coeff = FTC + MTC (always negative)
Power defect = ∫(power coeff) from HZP to HFP
Differential boron worth: less negative at higher temp & boron concentration
☣ Fission Product Poisons
▶
Xe-135: σₐ ≈ 2.6×10⁶ barns, t½ = 9.1 h
I-135: t½ = 6.6 h (Xe precursor, ~95% of Xe production)
Xe equilibrium: ~40–50 hours; independent of power at high flux
Post-trip Xe peak: ~10–11 hours
Sm-149: stable, σₐ ≈ 4×10⁴ barns
Pm-149: t½ = 53 h (Sm precursor)
Sm-149 equilibrium: ~25 days; independent of power
🔥 Thermodynamics
▶
P = ρgh (hydrostatic pressure)
Gauge = Absolute − Atmospheric
x = (h − hf)/(hg − hf) (quality)
η = 1 − Tcold/Thot (Carnot efficiency)
Q ∝ N, H ∝ N², P ∝ N³ (pump affinity laws)
LMTD = (ΔT₁ − ΔT₂)/ln(ΔT₁/ΔT₂)
DNBR = CHF/actual heat flux
